root_cause.text: The `StaxInInterceptor` improperly intercepted and processed `text/html` error messages on the server side instead of deferring to the standard server-side error handling mechanisms.
root_cause.evidence: The patch adds the `MessageUtils.isRequestor(message)` condition to the `text/html` content check, ensuring that the custom HTML error message reading logic is only executed on the client side.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The `StaxInInterceptor` improperly intercepted and processed `text/html` error messages on the server side instead of deferring to the standard server-side error handling mechanisms.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch adds the `MessageUtils.isRequestor(message)` condition to the `text/html` content check, ensuring that the custom HTML error message reading logic is only executed on the client side.

intent_record.what: Added an import statement for java.io.InputStreamReader.
intent_record.how: Makes the InputStreamReader class available for use within the file.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update imports by removing IOUtils and adding MessageUtils.
intent_record.what: Removed the import for IOUtils and added the import for MessageUtils.
intent_record.how: Updates the dependencies to reflect changes in the underlying class implementation.

intent_record.intent_summary: Improve HTML payload reading by increasing the size limit and ensuring it only applies to requestors.
intent_record.what: Replaced IOUtils.toString with a custom StringBuilder loop and added a check for MessageUtils.isRequestor.
intent_record.how: Increases the read limit for HTML messages to 64536 characters and restricts the execution of this logic to requestor messages.

grouping_result.intent: Update imports by removing IOUtils and adding MessageUtils.
Improve HTML payload reading by increasing the size limit and ensuring it only applies to requestors.

relevance_review.reason: The group only adds an import statement for java.io.InputStreamReader, which does not directly address the improper interception of text/html error messages in StaxInInterceptor.
The group only updates import statements, which is a refactoring change and does not directly address the logic for handling text/html error messages.

The group modifies the HTML payload reading logic to ensure it only applies to requestors, directly addressing the root cause where text/html error messages were improperly intercepted on the server side.

candidates.rationale: This check improperly intercepts text/html content unconditionally, without verifying if it is operating on the server side (requestor), leading to improper processing of HTML error messages.
This line reads the HTML payload unconditionally, which is part of the improper interception of text/html error messages.

vuln_statements.explanation: This condition unconditionally intercepts text/html content, leading to the improper processing of HTML error messages on the server side because it lacks a check to see if it is a requestor.
This line actually processes the intercepted HTML payload, which should have been bypassed for server-side error messages.

bic_trace_step.reason: The function `handleMessage` intercepts messages with `text/html` content types and unconditionally reads the content to throw a Fault (lines 65-75). It lacks checks to ensure this only happens on the client side (e.g., checking if the message is a requestor), which matches the vulnerability description.

The function contains the logic to intercept and process 'text/html' messages unconditionally (lines 65-75), which matches the root cause description of improperly handling HTML error messages instead of deferring them.
